question.title: How to avoid joining table when querying by a foreign key?
question.body:
I have a Comment class with a user property defined as:

    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
    @JoinColumn(name = "user_id")
    @NonNull
    private User user;

I have a CommentRepository:

public interface CommentRepository extends CrudRepository<Comment, Integer> {
    List<Comment> findByUserId(Integer userId);
}

I want to query a particular user's comments by his id.

I'm doing this:

commentRepository.findByUserId(userId);

everything works fine except the query looks like:

select
    comment0_."id" as id1_1_,
    comment0_."text" as url2_1_,
    comment0_."user_id" as user_id3_1_
    from
        "comments" comment0_
    left outer join
        "users" user1_
    on comment0_."user_id"=user1_."id"
    where
    user1_."id"=?

I want to avoid this join as I can query directly by the user_id column in a comments table.

I don't want to use a @Query annotation, I think there should be a smarter way.

answers.body:
I tried a few approaches and the only one that removes the inner join is:

@Column(name = "`user_id`", insertable = false, updatable = false)
private Long userId;

@ManyToOne(optional = false, fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
private User user;

Add an extra field userId (even getter/setter are not required). You do not require to set this field on create/update entity.

As a bonus it comes handy to add getter to fast access userId without fetching the User from the database: entity.getUserId() instead of entity.getUser().getId().

Note that it's not necessary to add @JoinColumn if you use conventional naming.

The default value for @ManyToOne(optional = true) and @JoinColumn(nullable = true) causes this extra join. You may try,

@ManyToOne(fetch=FetchType.LAZY, optional = false)
@JoinColumn(name="`user_id `", nullable = false)
